# fix(trust): PR#569 review findings — separator drift, legacy-sentinel disambiguation, envelope_id plumbing

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `fc97c952d4b6` — fix(trust): PR#569 review findings — separator drift, legacy-sentinel disambiguation, envelope_id plumbing

**Body**:

Addresses 2 MED + 1 MED + 3 LOW findings from the MUST-gate reviewer +
security-reviewer runs on PR #569 (terrene-foundation/kailash-py).

**MED (security) — _compute_chain_hash canonicalization drift**

src/kailash/trust/enforce/selective_disclosure.py::_compute_chain_hash
used default json.dumps separators (spaced), while AuditAnchor.compute_hash
uses compact separators per the cross-SDK contract (kailash-rs#449).
Semantically identical input produced different canonical forms between
the witness-export chain and the PACT audit-anchor chain — a Rust witness
verifier built against the GENESIS_HASH assumption would have failed.

Aligned all three json.dumps() sites in the module to the compact contract:
- _compute_chain_hash (line 267)
- export_for_witness sign_payload (line 330)
- verify_witness_export sign_payload (line 371) + chain_valid compare
  (lines 389-393)

All four use separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=True, default=str, sort_keys=True.

**MED (security) — verify_chain_integrity error message disambiguation**

src/kailash/trust/pact/audit.py::AuditChain.verify_chain_integrity emitted
"content hash mismatch (tampered?)" for BOTH real tampering AND legacy-
rooted chains (pre-2026-04-20 migration). Operators reading "(tampered?)"
during forensic triage on a pre-migration chain would reasonably conclude
an attack, when the actual action needed is re-seal.

Added AuditAnchor._compute_hash_legacy() helper that mirrors the pre-
migration contract (literal "genesis" sentinel + default json.dumps
separators). verify_chain_integrity() now tries the legacy compute when a
hash mismatch is found; if it matches, emits a distinct message:

  "Anchor N: legacy genesis sentinel detected — chain pre-dates 2026-04-20
   canonical alignment (kailash-rs#449). Re-seal required: re-invoke
   AuditAnchor.seal() across every anchor in the chain. This is NOT
   tampering."

Two new regression tests in test_audit_chain_genesis_canonical.py:
- test_chain_integrity_distinguishes_legacy_from_tampering
- test_chain_integrity_still_flags_real_tampering

**MED (code review) — envelope_id plumbing through _execute_supervised**

packages/kailash-pact/src/pact/engine.py::_execute_supervised previously
passed only agent_id=role to CostTracker.record(). The envelope_id kwarg
was added to CostTracker in the initial PR commit but never populated,
creating a soft orphan per rules/orphan-detection.md §1: the
consumption_report(envelope_id=...) future feature would see zero
populated rows from PactEngine-native callers.

Extracted envelope_id from verdict.effective_envelope_snapshot["id"] in
_submit_locked and plumbed it through _execute_supervised via a new
keyword-only `envelope_id` parameter. All three call sites (DISABLED
mode, SHADOW mode, ENFORCE mode) now pass the extracted envelope_id.
DISABLED mode passes None (no verdict exists).

**LOW (security) — DEBUG log operator comment on costs.py**

Added inline comment at packages/kailash-pact/src/pact/costs.py:102
noting that envelope_id and agent_id are operational identifiers (UUID +
D/T/R role address respectively) and MUST stay at DEBUG per
observability.md Rule 8 — any future refactor that upgrades to WARN
needs to re-verify the no-PII convention is preserved.

**LOW (code review) — thread-safety + bounded-deque regression tests**

Added TestThreadSafetyWithNewFields and TestBoundedDequeWrap classes in
packages/kailash-pact/tests/unit/test_cost_tracker_history_fields.py:
- test_concurrent_record_preserves_all_entries: 8 threads × 200 records
  each under the _lock, asserts no dropped/duplicated entries and that
  cumulative matches spent to 1e-9.
- test_history_wrap_preserves_new_fields: bounded deque eviction with
  envelope_id / agent_id set on every entry, asserts oldest records evict
  cleanly and retained records preserve all fields with monotone
  cumulative.

**LOW (code review) — docstring pointer on record(agent_id=...)**

costs.py::CostTracker.record() docstring now explicitly states the agent_id
convention (D/T/R role string, NOT kaizen-agents agent UUID) and warns
that downstream consumption_report(agent_id=...) consumers filtering by
UUID will get zero rows from PactEngine-populated trackers.

**Verification**: 2594 pact + pact-pkg tests pass (up from 2590 — 4 new
regression tests: thread-safety, deque wrap, legacy/tamper disambiguation
pair). 15 skipped, 4 pre-existing unrelated warnings (GovernanceContext
from_dict UserWarning on test_context.py, on main).

All mechanical sweeps pass:
- `grep -rn "'genesis'\|\"genesis\"" src/ packages/` — all remaining hits
  are EATP trust-chain dict keys for genesis records (different concept),
  not audit-chain sentinels.
- `grep -rn "\.record(" packages/kailash-pact/src/` — single production
  call site in engine.py now plumbs both envelope_id and agent_id.
- GENESIS_HASH exported in __all__ at src/kailash/trust/pact/audit.py.
- N4 conformance vector hash reproduces exactly via independent recompute.

Related: kailash-py #567 / #569, esperie/kailash-rs#449
